🚀 Kickoff Event · April 30, 2026
AI-Native
Companies
How Modern Businesses Are Actually Running on AI
📅 April 30, 2026 · 4:00 – 7:30 PM
📍 Life Time Work, Sandy Springs
👥 Founders, Executives, Operators
About This Session

This kickoff event launches the 2026 TechSeries by exploring how companies are transitioning from experimenting with AI tools to operating fully AI-enabled workflows across departments. We're moving past "we're exploring AI" into "AI is how we operate." This session shows you what that actually looks like — with real examples, real operators, and a 90-day plan you can take back to your organization.

What This Session Covers
What defines an AI-native company today Redesigning operations around AI Internal copilots vs autonomous workflows Real examples of AI-first systems Where leaders should start in the next 90 days AI stack decisions that matter
Evening Agenda
4:00 PMArrival + Networking — Vendor showcase, drinks, and pre-event connections
5:30 PMOpening Framing — Ethan Cortazzo sets the session context and frames the problem
5:35 PMFeatured Keynote — AI operating models in practice: a practitioner's perspective
5:50 PMExecutive Panel — Real companies, real numbers, real debate
6:20 PMBuilder Spotlight — Live workflow transformation case study
6:35 PMAudience Q&A — Direct access to all speakers
6:45 PMNetworking Reception — Continue conversations through 7:30 PM
Activation at This Event
AI Workflow Wall
Attendees share one process they want to automate
Sponsor Demo Stations
Hands-on with operational AI solutions
AI Maturity Assessment
QR survey — see where you stack up live
90-Day Takeaway Sheet
Walk out with an actionable plan

💰 Event 2 of 5 · June 18, 2026
The Economics
of AI
ROI, Costs, and the Scaling Reality Executives Need to Hear
📅 June 18, 2026 · 4:00 – 7:30 PM
📍 Life Time Work, Sandy Springs
👥 CEOs, CFOs, Operators, Product Leaders
About This Session

Executives are moving beyond experimentation and asking the real question: does AI actually make financial sense? This session tackles the financial, infrastructure, and operational realities of scaling AI inside organizations — from the true cost of building vs buying, to how to measure ROI in a way your CFO will believe, to the hidden expenses that kill AI initiatives before they deliver.

What This Session Covers
Why most AI initiatives fail financially How to measure ROI on AI investments Infrastructure costs and vendor tradeoffs Real cost savings and revenue growth examples How to build a business case leadership approves Build vs buy vs partner decisions
Evening Agenda
4:00 PMNetworking + Vendor Showcase — Connect with analytics and infrastructure partners
5:30 PMWelcome + Framing — Ethan Cortazzo frames the economics conversation
5:35 PMKeynote — The financial truth about AI adoption
5:50 PMPanel: Case Studies — Cost realities from operators who've been through it
6:20 PMOperator Presentation — Before/after ROI breakdown from a real company
6:40 PMQ&A + Closing Insights
6:45 PMNetworking Reception — Through 7:30 PM

"What's your biggest AI investment concern?"

👥 Event 3 of 5 · August 20, 2026
Building
AI-Ready Teams
Talent, Culture & Leadership in the AI Era
📅 August 20, 2026 · 4:00 – 7:30 PM
📍 Life Time Work, Sandy Springs
👥 Executives, HR Leaders, Founders
About This Session

As AI reshapes workflows, companies must rethink hiring, leadership, and organizational design from the ground up. This session focuses on the human side of transformation — managing adoption resistance, redefining roles that no longer exist in their original form, building culture that embraces AI, and preparing your workforce for the next 3–5 years of accelerating change.

What This Session Covers
How AI changes team structure and hiring Skills that matter in AI-enabled companies Managing adoption resistance Leadership strategies for AI transformation Workforce planning for 2027 and beyond Culture design for AI-first orgs
Evening Agenda
4:00 PMNetworking + Refreshments — HR and leadership-focused vendor showcase
5:30 PMOpening Remarks — Setting the people-first AI transformation context
5:35 PMLeadership Keynote — Building an AI-first organizational culture
5:50 PMPanel — Real organizational shifts happening inside companies right now
6:20 PMFounder Perspective — Building an AI-first team from scratch
6:40 PMAudience Discussion — Interactive prompts and group conversation
6:45 PMNetworking Reception — Through 7:30 PM

Interactive visual — map your future team structure

⚙️ Event 4 of 5 · October 22, 2026
Autonomous
Business Systems
From Automation to AI Agents Running Your Business
📅 October 22, 2026 · 4:00 – 7:30 PM
📍 Life Time Work, Sandy Springs
👥 Tech Leaders, Ops Teams, Founders
About This Session

The next frontier isn't just automation — it's autonomous workflows and AI agents handling multi-step business processes without human intervention. This event explores what that actually means in practice today: what companies are building, what's working, what the governance and risk considerations are, and how to prepare your infrastructure for the shift from reactive automation to proactive autonomous systems.

What This Session Covers
What agentic AI really means for business Real autonomous workflows in production today Where automation ends and AI agents begin Risks, controls, and governance frameworks Infrastructure requirements for agentic AI How to start your first agent deployment
Evening Agenda
4:00 PMNetworking + Vendor Showcase — Automation and agent platform demos
5:30 PMWelcome Framing — The shift from automation to autonomy
5:35 PMKeynote — The rise of autonomous workflows in enterprise
5:50 PMPanel — Practical implementation stories from operators
6:20 PMBuilder Demo — Live AI agent use case walkthrough
6:40 PMClosing Insights — What to build first in your organization
6:45 PMNetworking Reception — Through 7:30 PM

Hands-on with live AI agent workflows

The Team

The People Behind
TechSeries.ai

Produced by the Getnetworked team — community builders, event professionals, and operators passionate about Atlanta's AI ecosystem.

Ethan Cortazzo
Founder & Series Host
Ethan is the founder of Networked AI — the Atlanta-based community platform powering TechSeries.ai. A connector at heart and a builder by nature, Ethan has spent years at the intersection of business strategy and emerging technology, helping executives cut through AI hype to find real operational leverage.

As host and curator of TechSeries.ai, Ethan brings together Atlanta's top operators, founders, and technology leaders for events that prioritize substance over spectacle. Known for direct conversation, actionable takeaways, and a community that shows up ready to work.

When he's not running events, Ethan works directly with organizations on AI adoption strategy — translating cutting-edge capabilities into practical business systems that create real competitive advantage.

About TechSeries.ai

Atlanta's Home for
AI Business Execution

TechSeries.ai is the flagship event series from Getnetworked — Atlanta's community platform for AI business leaders. We produce five curated evenings per year at Life Time Work in Sandy Springs, bringing together the region's top operators, founders, and executives.

Our events aren't panels about AI's potential. They're rooms full of people actively implementing it — sharing real numbers, real failures, and real frameworks. Every session follows a structured program: keynote, executive panel, builder spotlight, and meaningful networking.

The 2026 series follows a deliberate arc: from understanding what AI-native companies actually look like, through the economics of scaling AI, to building the teams and systems that can run autonomously. It culminates in an Executive Summit that will set the agenda for Atlanta's AI community in 2027.

Ethan Cortazzo
Founder · Networked AI
Our Mission

We exist to close the gap between AI hype and AI execution.

Most business leaders know AI is transforming their industry. Far fewer have a clear plan to implement it inside their organization. TechSeries.ai is where those leaders get real answers — from peers who've already done the work.

Founding events

What started as a three-part local series became the foundation of Atlanta's most engaged AI business community. The 2026 series builds directly on this momentum with a bigger stage, deeper content, and broader reach.

✓ Completed · Series 3 of 3

The Future of AI
& Business in 2026

February 19, 2026 · 4:00–7:30 PM · Life Time Work Sandy Springs
Overview

The series finale. This session focused on how AI is transitioning from experimentation into execution — and what leaders must prepare for as AI becomes core business infrastructure in 2026 and beyond. Not hype. Not theory. Execution.

Program
4:00 PMArrival, Registration & Networking — Vendor showcase, bar, light refreshments
5:25 PMWelcome & Framing — Ethan Cortazzo · Tech Series recap & evening context
5:30 PMStrategic Opening Address — Kwanza Hall, Chairman, Development Authority of Fulton County · "The Infrastructure Powering AI's Next Decade"
5:40 PMFeatured Panel: The Future of AI & Business in 2026 — Erich Starrett, Dr. Beverly Wright, Mark Michelson, Aylin Orial · Moderated by Ethan Cortazzo
6:10 PMFeatured Keynote — Aby Varma, Spark Novus · "From Experimentation to Advantage: Scaling AI That Works"
6:30 PMMicro Keynote — Stephanie Richards, SowGrow PR · "Visibility in the Age of AI Search"
6:40 PMBuilder Spotlight — Sree Pradhip & Ameet Shedge · "From AI Ideas to Real Execution"
7:00 PMNetworking Reception — Speaker engagement, sponsor conversations, community building

AI in the Workplace:
Productivity & Automation

December 18, 2025 · 4:00–7:00 PM · Life Time Work Sandy Springs
Overview

Part 2 of the founding series. This session explored how leaders use AI to enhance productivity, streamline workflows, and empower teams to perform at peak efficiency. After a packed house for Responsible AI, the momentum continued with a dynamic evening of panels, micro keynotes, and a live DJ social closing reception.

Responsible AI
& Best Practices

October 30, 2025 · 4:00–7:00 PM · Life Time Work Sandy Springs
Overview

The event that started it all. The kickoff session explored how organizations can adopt artificial intelligence in ways that build trust, protect customers, and drive sustainable growth. A full afternoon of curated coworking, thought leadership, and high-value networking — featuring a global AI governance authority as headliner.

FAQ

Common Questions

How much do events cost?+
Events are $15–20 per person and include coworking access, light bites, and the full networking reception. The Executive Summit may have a different pricing structure when announced.
Who should attend TechSeries.ai events?+
Founders, executives, operators, consultants, and technology leaders who are actively thinking about AI adoption — whether you're just getting started or already deploying at scale. If you have a real decision to make about AI, this room is for you.
Where are events held?+
All 2026 events are held at Life Time Work Sandy Springs — 5600 Roswell Rd Building C, Sandy Springs, GA 30342. It's one of Atlanta's most premium collaborative workspaces and provides free parking on site.
How can I speak at a TechSeries.ai event?+
We feature practitioners with real AI implementation results — not keynote-circuit speakers. If your organization has deployed AI systems, driven measurable ROI, or built AI-powered workflows, submit a speaking inquiry through our Sponsors page and we'll be in touch.
How can my company sponsor an event?+
We offer single-event sponsorships ($2,500), full series title sponsorships (custom pricing), and community partnership packages. Sponsorship includes vendor showcase space, logo placement, complimentary tickets, and access to opted-in attendee contacts. Visit our Sponsors page to submit an inquiry.
Is TechSeries.ai affiliated with Getnetworked?+
Yes — TechSeries.ai is the flagship event series from Getnetworked, Atlanta's AI business community platform. All registrations and event management run through the Getnetworked platform at net-worked.ai.
What is the Executive Summit in December?+
The Executive Summit is the flagship year-end gathering — a premium event bringing together Atlanta's top AI business leaders to review the year and set the agenda for 2027. Date, venue, and format are to be announced. Join the priority waitlist via our Register page to get first access when it drops.
